# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write an Istio AuthorizationPolicy that controls access to services. Create rules to allow traffic from specific service accounts. Configure source and operation matching for HTTP methods and paths. This is for our microservices access control.
# Category: istio_authz_bypass

apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: microservices-access-policy
  namespace: default # Replace with the actual namespace where your microservices run
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-service # Apply this policy to services with the label app: my-service
      # Use a broader selector like `app: "*"` to apply to all services in the namespace
      # or specify multiple services:
      # app:
      #   in: ["service-a", "service-b"]
  rules:
  # Rule 1: Allow product-viewer service account to read product information
  - from:
    - source:
        principals:
        - "cluster.local/ns/default/sa/product-viewer-sa" # Replace with the actual service account principal
    to:
    - operation:
        methods: ["GET", "HEAD"]
        paths: ["/api/v1/products", "/api/v1/products/*"] # Allow GET/HEAD on /api/v1/products and its sub-paths
    action: ALLOW
  
  # Rule 2: Allow order-processor service account to create and update orders
  - from:
    - source:
        principals:
        - "cluster.local/ns/default/sa/order-processor-sa" # Replace with the actual service account principal
    to:
    - operation:
        methods: ["POST"]
        paths: ["/api/v1/orders"] # Allow POST to create new orders
    - operation:
        methods: ["PUT", "PATCH"]
        paths: ["/api/v1/orders/*"] # Allow PUT/PATCH to update specific orders
    action: ALLOW

  # Rule 3: Allow admin service account full access to admin endpoints
  - from:
    - source:
        principals:
        - "cluster.local/ns/default/sa/admin-sa" # Replace with the actual service account principal
    to:
    - operation:
        methods: ["*"] # Allow all HTTP methods
        paths: ["/api/v1/admin", "/api/v1/admin/*"] # Allow all operations on /api/v1/admin and its sub-paths
    action: ALLOW
